Technical Guide

What Is a Tech Pack? The Clothing Brand's Complete Guide

✎ FABRIOZA Team 📅 July 1, 2026 ☕ 11 min read
Home / Blog / What Is a Tech Pack? The Clothing Brand's Complete Guide

A tech pack is the most important document in clothing manufacturing. It's the blueprint that tells your factory exactly what to make, how to make it, and what materials to use. A good tech pack eliminates miscommunication, reduces sample rounds, and saves you time and money. This guide explains everything you need to know — and when you can skip it.

What Is a Tech Pack?

A tech pack (technical pack) is a detailed specification document that includes everything a manufacturer needs to produce your garment. Think of it as the architectural blueprint for your clothing.

What a Tech Pack Contains

1. Technical Flat Sketch: Front and back line drawing showing every seam, pocket, zipper, and detail. This visual reference eliminates "I thought you meant..." conversations.

2. Measurement Spec Sheet: Point of measurement (POM) specifications for every dimension: chest width, body length, sleeve length, shoulder width, neck opening, etc. — listed for every size.

3. Bill of Materials (BOM): Complete list of every material: main fabric (type, weight, color, composition), lining, trims (buttons, zippers, drawstrings, elastic), thread, labels, and packaging.

4. Construction Details: How the garment is assembled — stitch types, seam allowances, hem finishes, pocket construction, reinforcement points.

5. Artwork and Placement: Print/embroidery designs with exact placement measurements: "logo 3 inches from center front, 2 inches below neckline."

6. Color Specifications: Pantone codes for every color. Lab dips (fabric color samples) for approval.

Why Manufacturers Need Tech Packs

Without a tech pack, manufacturers must interpret your vision from descriptions or photos. This leads to: incorrect measurements, wrong fabric weights, misplaced artwork, inconsistent sizing, and more sample rounds (costing time and money). A tech pack eliminates 90% of these issues.

Do You Need a Tech Pack to Order From FABRIOZA?

Honest answer: No, not for standard styles. For basic garments (t-shirts, hoodies, joggers, standard sportswear), we can work from:

  • Reference garments: Send us a piece you love, we'll replicate and modify
  • Sketches or photos: Our design team interprets your vision
  • Detailed descriptions: "Boxy fit, 280gsm cotton, dropped shoulder, chest embroidery"

For fully custom or complex garments, a tech pack ensures accuracy. Download our free tech pack template.

How Much Does a Tech Pack Cost?

Professional tech pack services charge $100-500 per garment. At FABRIOZA, we help develop basic tech pack specifications from your references at no charge for orders of 50+ pieces.

Who Creates Tech Packs?

  • Professional technical designers: Most accurate, highest cost
  • Your manufacturer (FABRIOZA): Moderate detail, often free with order
  • DIY with templates: Use our free template to create your own

Tech Pack for Common Garments

GarmentTech Pack Needed?Alternative
Standard T-shirtNoDesign reference + description
Custom HoodieNo (for basic styles)Reference garment + GSM preference
JoggersNo (for basic styles)Reference garment + fit notes
Team JerseyHelpfulDesign file + sizing
Fully Custom JacketYesRequired for accuracy

Our team builds the specification from your reference. Free consultation. Quote in 24 hours.

Frequently Asked Questions

Can I order without a tech pack?

Yes, for standard styles. Send us design references, photos, or descriptions and our team creates production specs for you.

How detailed does a tech pack need to be?

More detail = fewer errors. At minimum: measurements, fabric specs, color references, and artwork placement.

Can FABRIOZA create a tech pack for me?

Yes, we develop production specifications from your references at no charge for 50+ piece orders.

What software should I use for tech packs?

Adobe Illustrator for sketches, Excel for spec sheets. Or use our free template as a starting point.

Do I need a tech pack for every size?

One tech pack per style covers all sizes. We grade the pattern from your base size to all other sizes.
